evidence: Multi-bill is integrated into the Bill Home and Checkout flows where users expect to pay multiple services. Entry points are consistent with Single Bill.
Bundle Order implementation uses a single primary CTA for the total payment, preventing double-submit risk via PE idempotency layer.
PayLater limit must sync immediately across surfaces. Risk identified in C40 doc regarding refund destination (Wallet vs PayLater limit). For credit products, refund must ideally revert to limit.
Fees for PayLater (if any) are calculated on the total Bundle Order amount and displayed at the cashier before confirmation.
Every sub-order and the Bundle Order are mapped to FMC ledger entries. Distribution logic ensures financial reconciliation for each merchant.
In-product dispute path for specific sub-orders within a Bundle needs to be clearly defined in the Transaction Detail. Currently, it might only show at the Bundle level.
PayLater is now available as a source of fund for Multi-bill (C40 upgrade), removing previous restrictions.
Result screen distinguishes between total success and partial success for independent bundles, providing clear status per sub-order.
Bundle ID and sub-order IDs are generated and visible in Transaction History for reference.
PayLater specific errors (Insufficient Limit, Account Locked) are passed from PE to the client with descriptive messages.
Anti-duplication is enforced at the Bundle Order level using the requestID from the client.
Idempotency is maintained for both the initial deduction and the subsequent fund distribution to sub-orders.
